title: CUDA complex types use precision-bearing cf32 and cf64 aliases
summary: cuFloatComplex and cuComplex display as cf32, while cuDoubleComplex displays as cf64, with CUDA provenance retained through seafoam color.
details: The exact mappings are cuFloatComplex -> cf32, cuComplex -> cf32, and cuDoubleComplex -> cf64.
The mapping applies in declarations, parameters, templates, raw expressions, and moved C-style return types without changing source bytes.
Exact identifier boundaries prevent an alias from rewriting only the prefix of a longer project identifier.
CUDA's installed cuComplex.h defines cuFloatComplex from float2, cuDoubleComplex from double2, and cuComplex as a typedef of cuFloatComplex.
No corresponding cuFloat or cuDouble scalar typedefs exist in that header; ordinary C++ float and double already display as f32 and f64.
Seafoam indicates CUDA API/type provenance, not whether a particular object or value resides in device memory.
rationale: cf32/cf64 communicate complex shape and precision in four characters, while the semantic color retains the library information removed with cu.
A single ordered alias table prevents overlay and raw-line paths from drifting.
alternatives: FloatComplex/DoubleComplex after hiding only cu; rejected by the owner as less compact than the precision-bearing spelling.
Invent cuFloat/cuDouble scalar aliases; rejected because those CUDA typedefs do not exist and native float/double already have f32/f64 presentation.

provenance.note: Owner selected cf32/cf64 and required the resulting aliases to remain CUDA green.
Installed CUDA header establishes the float, double, and compatibility typedef relationships and contains no scalar cuFloat/cuDouble types.
The ordered exact alias table is consumed by structured and raw render paths.

evidence.claim: Both precisions, the compatibility alias, CUDA color, raw templates, overlays, and source integrity are pinned.

evidence.reference: style_lab/catalog.json cuda-api scene and real ext-linegrid capture
evidence.claim: Actual Neovim RGB output shows cf32 in CUDA seafoam.

questions: Why are cf32 and cf64 green?
Are there cuFloat and cuDouble scalar variants?

last_verified.method: Owner decision, installed-header inspection, focused extmark tests, interaction round-trip, and actual ext-linegrid RGB capture.
